name: Ainsa along the Ara River — colada of the Barranco de las Guargas
name_original: Aínsa por el Río Ara — colada del Barranco de las Guargas
region_intro: Touring cycling around Boltaña offers diverse landscapes within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by mountains, forests, and the undammed Ara River. The region provides access to protected areas such as Ordesa and Monte Perdido National Park and Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, featuring impressive mountains and rugged canyons. Cycling routes often connect to nearby villages and historical sites, with varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Boltaña The most popular touring cycling route is Ainsa along the Ara River — colada of the Barranco de las Guargas, an 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Ara River, connecting to the historic town of Aínsa. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Boltaña Monastery loop from Boltaña, an easy 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This loop starts and ends in Boltaña, passing by the historic Boltaña Monastery. Local touring cyclists also love the Las Margas Trail – Torrelisa loop from Boltaña, a 28.4 miles (45.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Boltaña is defined by mountainous landscapes, river valleys, and access to natural parks, offering options for different ability levels from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Boltaña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Boltaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Boltaña?
A: Boltaña offers a diverse selection of around 15 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy riverside rides to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for families in Boltaña?
A: Yes, Boltaña has easy touring cycling routes perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975165071/ainsa-along-the-ara-river-colada-of-the-barranco-de-las-guargas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ainsa along the Ara River — colada of the Barranco de las Guargas&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 11.2-mile (18.0 km) trail that follows the scenic Ara River, connecting to the historic town of Aínsa. Another gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boltaña Monastery loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 10.4-mile (16.8 km) path that passes by the historic Boltaña Monastery.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on touring cycling routes in Boltaña?
A: Touring cycling routes in Boltaña showcase the stunning natural beauty of the Aragonese Pyrenees. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, lush forests, and the pristine Ara River, one of the few undammed Pyrenean rivers. The region also provides access to protected areas like Ordesa and Monte Perdido National Park, known for its impressive mountains and deep gorges, and Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park with its rugged canyons.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Boltaña?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes in Boltaña are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boltaña Monastery loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 10.4-mile (16.8 km) ride. For a more challenging option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Margas Trail – Torrelisa loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 28.4-mile (45.7 km) trail.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to explore near the cycling routes?
A: The Boltaña area is rich in history and natural beauty. Along or near cycling routes, you can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4594760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aínsa Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Losa Mora&lt;/a&gt;, or see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful Abandoned Sanctuary of San Urbez&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3486403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Aínsa and Peña Montañesa from Partara Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is highly recommended.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Boltaña?
A: The best time to go touring cycling in Boltaña is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations, making some routes less accessible.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Boltaña offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24315408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Moscarales – Ferrera Viewpoint loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.1-mile (38.9 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Margas Trail – Torrelisa loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 28.4 miles (45.7 km) through varied and often unpaved terrain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Boltaña?
A: The touring cycling routes in Boltaña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the quiet roads, and the opportunity to explore both natural wonders and historical sites.

Q: Are there options for wild swimming along touring cycling routes in Boltaña?
A: Yes, the pristine Ara River, which flows near Boltaña, offers opportunities for wild swimming. The Gorga natural pool along the river is a particularly scenic spot, perfect for a refreshing dip and a picnic during your cycling tour.

Q: Is there public transport available in Boltaña that accommodates bicycles?
A: While specific public transport options for bicycles can vary, Boltaña is well-connected by local bus services. It's advisable to check with the local transport providers directly regarding their policies for carrying bicycles, especially for longer journeys or during peak seasons.

Q: Where can I find parking for my bike in Boltaña?
A: Boltaña, being a cycling-friendly area, offers various public parking options. You'll typically find designated parking areas within the town, especially near the main attractions and starting points for popular routes. Many accommodations also provide secure bike storage for their guests.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Boltaña?
A: Boltaña and its surrounding villages, such as Aínsa, offer charming cafes and traditional pubs where cyclists can take a break, refuel, and enjoy local refreshments. Many routes pass through or near these villages, providing convenient stops.

Q: Can I rent a bike or get repairs done in Boltaña?
A: Yes, Boltaña and the wider Zona Zero region are well-equipped for cyclists. You can find bike rental services and repair shops in the area, particularly in Boltaña itself and nearby Aínsa, ensuring you have access to necessary equipment and maintenance during your tour.
tips: The views from the bridge over the confluence of the Ara and Cinca are unforgettable. From this bridge you can see how the waters enter the Mediano reservoir and how they flow from the valleys dominated by the Peña Montañesa.

The Catillo de Aínsa is a fortification that is located on the highest part of the hill from where the town is located. This castle offers a spectacular view of the upper part of Aínsa, the Graus marsh and the mountains of the Sierra Ferrera, with Peña Montañosa as its flag.

waypoints.name: View of the Cinca River and Peña Montañesa
View of Aínsa from the castle
